title: Student — Courses & Enrolment
content:
Overview


The Courses section is where students access their enrolled school subjects and browse additional self-study courses. It is divided into two pages: My Courses and All Courses. The Enrolment page is where students request to join teacher-led subjects.



My Courses Page


My Courses shows all subjects the student is currently enrolled in through the school. Each course card displays the subject name. Clicking a course card opens the course detail view with six resource types:


  • Textbook: Comprehensive study material organized by chapters
  • Examination Quiz: Full-length practice exams in real exam format
  • Topic Quiz: Quick quizzes focused on specific topics within the subject
  • Flashcards: Interactive flashcards for key concepts and terminology
  • Video Lessons: Video tutorials and explanations for the subject
  • Past Questions & Answers: Previous exam questions with detailed solutions

Clicking any resource opens it within the course detail page. A back button returns to the course overview. There is also a Back to My Courses button to return to the full course list.


If the student is not yet enrolled in any courses, an empty state message encourages them to browse available courses or contact their teacher.



All Courses Page


All Courses displays every self-study course available on the platform — over 30 courses across physics, mathematics, chemistry, biology, economics, and SAT preparation. Key features:


  • Search bar: Filter courses by name as you type
  • Course count: Shows the total number of available courses

Each course card shows:


  • Course name
  • Description: Topics covered and level (e.g., "College-level physics covering kinematics, dynamics...")
  • Enroll Now button: Click to enroll and add the course to My Courses

Some courses may show a "Coming Soon" badge if they are not yet available. The Enroll Now button will be disabled for these.


Available courses include:


  • Physics: AP Physics 1, AP Physics 2, AP Physics C (Mechanics), AP Physics C (E&M), Physics Grades 9–12
  • Mathematics: Algebra 1 & 2, Geometry, Pre-Calculus, AP Calculus AB & BC
  • Chemistry: Chemistry Grades 10–12
  • Biology: Biology Grades 9–12, AP Biology, Biology I & II
  • Economics: High School Economics, AP Macroeconomics, AP Microeconomics, Macroeconomics First Year, International Trade and Finance, International Economics
  • Test Prep: SAT Preparation


Enrolment Page


The Enrolment page is where students request to join teacher-led subjects at their school. It has two main sections:


Available Courses:


  • Shows all subjects created by teachers at the school
  • Filter by Term and Year to find subjects for a specific semester
  • Search by subject name
  • Each course card has a Request Enrolment button
  • The section is collapsible — click the header to expand or collapse

My Enrolment Requests:


  • Shows all requests the student has submitted
  • Each request displays the subject name, status (Pending, Enrolled, or Rejected), and date
  • Pending requests are awaiting teacher approval
  • Enrolled means the teacher approved the request and the student can now access the subject in My Courses
  • Rejected requests can be deleted by the student


Key Talking Points for Agents


  • "My Courses shows subjects your teachers have approved. All Courses shows everything available for self-study."
  • "Click a course card to see all six resource types — textbook, quizzes, flashcards, videos, and past questions."
  • "Use the Enrolment page to request access to your teacher's subjects. They'll approve or reject from their portal."
  • "If you don't see a subject in Available Courses, check that you're looking at the right term and year."
  • "You can delete rejected enrolment requests to clean up your list."
